item.title: Golden Rule of Rebasing
item.description: Golden Rule of Rebasing The Golden Rule of Rebasing in Git | Axosoft (gitkraken.com) Quote The Golden Rule of Rebasing reads: “Never rebase while you’re on a public branch.” Or rather, ...

item.title: Git Maintainance

item.title: Upstream and Remote-Tracking Branches
item.description: Upstream and Remote-Tracking Branches Upstream branches are the branches on the remote repository e.g. main Remote tracking branches are the branches on the local repository which mirrors their corresponding upstream branches e.g.

item.title: Traversing Parent Commits
item.description: Traversing Commit What’s the difference between HEAD^ and HEAD~ in Git? - Stack Overflow ~2 means up two levels in the hierarchy Via the first parent if a commit has more than one parent ...

item.title: Editing Past Commits
item.description: Editing Past Commits Imagine we have a commit graph like this: A---B---C (feature, origin/feature) and we want to edit the commit B. Rebasing a Temporary Branch amending a single file in a past commit in git - Stack Overflow Create a temporary branch e.g.

item.title: Git Hooks
item.description: Git Hooks Git - Git Hooks (git-scm.com) Git hooks are scripts that are executed on specific events e.g. pre-commit, pre-push. By default, hooks are stored under .git/hooks, and Git executes the script with the file name corresponding to the event e.g.

item.title: Git Internals
item.description: Git Internals Git - Git Objects (git-scm.com) There are three types of git objects: Blob objects Tree objects Commit objects Unlike csv or subversion which keeps track of changes, ...
